commentaries.author: Augustine of Hippo
commentaries.content: 1 Esdras Wisdom of Solomon Wisdom of Sirach Epistle of Jeremiah Prayer of Manasseh Consider my affliction, and deliver me: for I do not forget your law. Augustine of Hippo 151. Let no man, set in Christ's body, imagine these words to be alien from himself, since in truth it is the whole body of Christ placed in this humble state that speaketh: "O consider my humiliation, and deliver me: for I forget not Thy law" (ver. In this place we cannot understand any law of God so suitably, as that whereby it is immutably determined that "every one that exalteth himself, shall be abased; and every one that humbleth himself; shall be exalted.

commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: Humiliation. Or "humility "as St. Augustine understands it of that virtue. (Calmet)

advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
